description: Attacks crafted via gradients from our own Supervisor, tested against targets with zero involvement in crafting
key_finding: Across all three black-box targets, degradation is dramatically weaker than the corresponding white-box results (60-95% F1 drops), confirming poor cross-model transferability of attacks crafted against a single CNN-based target. The one notable exception is SEAR against Mantra-Net specifically (39.3% drop), plausibly linked to Mantra-Net's noise-anomaly-sensitive architecture (Bayar constrained convolutions). Several near-zero or slightly negative 'drops' likely reflect sampling noise on already-low, noisy baseline F1 scores (0.04-0.09 range) at N=50, rather than genuine attack-induced improvement.
